lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need another assignment.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Excellent, you're doing great. Your new task is to kill [amount] [task monster].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got any tips for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, great!

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Good luck! Don't forget to come back when you need a new assignment.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: You're still hunting [task monster]; you have [amount] to go. Come back when you've finished your task.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: You're still hunting [monster], with [amount] to go.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: I don't think that's a suitable task for you. Shall I cancel it? This will not wipe your task streaks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please cancel it.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Alright, consider the task cancelled. You can now get a new assignment when you want one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thanks, I want to try doing it.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Good luck with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any rewards for me, or anything to trade?

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: I have quite a few rewards you can earn, and a wide variety of Slayer equipment for sale.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about the difficulty of my assignments.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: The Slayer Masters may currently assign you any task in our lists, regardless of your combat level.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's fine - I can handle any task.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: That's the spirit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In future, please don't give anything too tough.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Okay, from now on, all the Slayer Masters will take your combat level into account when choosing tasks for you, so you shouldn't get anything too hard.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: The Slayer Masters will take your combat level into account when choosing tasks for you, so you shouldn't get anything too hard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's fine - I don't want anything too tough.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Okay, we'll keep checking your combat level.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop checking my combat level - I can take anything!

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Okay, from now on, all the Slayer Masters will assign you anything from their lists, regardless of your combat level.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Nothing...

sections.title: Before the update Game Improvements (26 March 2020)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: 'Ello, who's that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [player name]. I'm using my magic to communicate with you.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Bizarre, go on, what do you want then?

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: 'Ello, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Let's get started by teaching you to wield a weapon.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Very good, but that little butter knife isn't going to protect you much. Let's get you something a bit better. Once you're properly equipped we can move on to some actual combat.

sections.title: Before equipping bronze sword and wooden shield

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Let's get you equipped.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: You should check out the combat interface.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Time for some actual combat! Head into the pen and kill one of those rats.

sections.title: After killing a rat with melee

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did it! I killed a giant rat!

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: I saw. You seem better at this than I thought. Now that you have grasped basic swordplay, let's move on.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Next up we have ranged attacking. With this you can kill foes from a distance. With ranged, you'll be able to attack the rats without entering the pit meaning they won't be able to fight back.

sections.title: Before killing a rat with ranged

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Use your bow and arrows to kill one of those rats from outside the pen.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about combat stats again.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Certainly. You have three main combat stats: Strength, Defence and Attack.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Strength determines the maximum hit you will be able to deal with your blows, Defence determines the amount of damage you will be able to defend and Attack determines the accuracy of your blows.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Other stats are used in combat such as Prayer, Hitpoints, Magic and Ranged. All of these stats can go towards determining your combat level, which is shown near the top of your combat interface screen.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: You will find out on the mainland that certain items can also affect your stats. There are potions that can be drunk that can alter your stats temporarily, such as raising Strength.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: You will also raise your Defence and Attack values by using different weapons and armours.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Before going into combat with an opponent it is wise to put the mouse over them and see what combat level they are.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Green coloured writing usually means it will be an easy fight for you, red means you will probably lose, yellow means they are around your level and orange means they are slightly stronger.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Sometimes things will go your way, sometimes they won't. There is no such thing as a guaranteed win, but if the odds are on your side, you stand the best chance of walking away victorious.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Now, was there something else you wanted to hear about again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about melee combat again.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Ah, my speciality. Close combat fighting, which is also known as melee fighting, covers all combat done at close range to your opponent.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Melee fighting depends entirely upon your three basic combat stats: Attack, Defence, and Strength.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Also, of course, it depends on the quality of your armour and weaponry. A high-levelled fighter with good armour and weapons will be deadly up close.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: If this is the path you wish to take, remember your success depends on getting as close to your enemy as quickly as possible.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Personally, I consider the fine art of melee combat to be the ONLY combat method worth using.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Now, did you want to hear anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about ranged combat again.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Thinking of being a ranger, eh? Well, okay. I don't enjoy it myself, but I can see the appeal.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Ranging employs a lot of different weapons as a skill, not just the shortbow you have there. Spears, throwing knives, and crossbows are all used best at a distance from your enemy.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Now, those rats were easy pickings, but on the mainland you will be very lucky if you can find a spot where you can shoot at your enemies without them being able to retaliate.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: At close range, rangers often do badly in combat. Your best tactic as a ranger is to hit and run, keeping your foe at a distance.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Your effectiveness as a ranger is almost entirely dependent on your Ranged stat. As with all skills, the more you train it, the more powerful it will become.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Anything else you wanted to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Wilderness again.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Ah yes, the Wilderness. It is a place of evil, mark my words. Many is the colleague I have lost in that foul place.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: It is also a place of both adventure and wealth, so if you are brave enough and strong enough to survive it, you will make a killing. Literally!

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: It is also the only place in the land of Gielinor where players are able to attack each other at will, and as such is the haunt of many Player Killers, or PKers if you will.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: There are a few things different in the Wilderness in comparison to the rest of the lands of Gielinor. Firstly, as I just mentioned, you can and will be attacked by other players.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: For this reason, you will be given a warning when you approach the Wilderness, as it is not a place you would wish to enter by accident.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Secondly, there are a number of 'levels' to it. The further into it you travel, the greater the range of people you can attack.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: In level 1 wilderness you will only be able to attack, or be attacked by, those players within one combat level of yourself.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: In level 50, any player within fifty levels of you will be able to attack, or be attacked by you. Always keep an eye on what level of the Wilderness you are currently in.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Your current Wilderness level is shown at the bottom right of the screen. The final thing you should know about the Wilderness is being 'skulled'.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: If you attack another player without them having attacked first, you will gain a skull above your character's head.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: What this means is that if you die while skulled, you will lose EVERYTHING that your character was carrying.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: When skulled, you should try to avoid dying for the twenty minutes or so it will take for the skull to wear off.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: If you wish to find the Wilderness, head north from where you start on the mainland. It is rather large and hard to miss.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: If you don't wish to end up there, take notice of the warning you will receive when getting near to it.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Now, was there anything more?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, I'm ready to move on!

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi! My name's [player name].

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: Do I look like I care? To me you're just another newcomer who thinks they're ready to fight.

lines.speaker: Combat Instructor
lines.text: I am Vannaka, the greatest swordsman alive, and I'm here to teach you the basics of combat. Let's get started by teaching you to wield a weapon.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Here, have this.

lines.speaker: Vannaka
lines.text: Here, have this!
